opinion.h4: WAGGONER ET AL. v. ROSENN, SECRETARY OF PUBLIC WELFARE OF PENNSYLVANIA. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE MIDDLE DISTRICT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 144, Misc. Decided May 5, 1969.
opinion.p: William C. Sennett, Attorney General of Pennsylvania, and Edgar R. Casper, Deputy Attorney General, for appellee.

The motion of appellants for leave to proceed in forma pauperis is granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded to the United States District Court for the Middle District of Pennsylvania for further consideration in light of Shapiro v. Thompson, ante, p. 618.
394 U.S. 846 RHODES v. KANSAS. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE DISTRICT COURT OF SEDGWICK COUNTY, KANSAS. No. 877, Misc. Decided May 5, 1969.
Kent Frizzell, Attorney General of Kansas, and E. G. Collister, Jr., Deputy Attorney General, for respondent.

The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for a writ of certiorari are granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded to the District Court of Sedgwick County, Kansas, for further consideration in light of Smith v. Hooey, 393 U.S. 374.
